380	          ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') := rfl
381	    have h4 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
382	        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' = f.c := by
383	      have : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
384	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' =

660	    -- projection). So the goal is `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0`, i.e.
661	    -- `hd_app`.
662	    exact hd_app
663	  · -- Sub-claim `h_exact` : the short complex is exact at the middle term.
664	    --
665	    -- Strategy.
666	    -- Same ring-level input as `h_zero`: `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`
667	    -- states `Function.Exact (mapBaseChange R A B) (map R A B B)`, i.e. range equals kernel.
668	    -- For the sheaf-of-modules short complex, exactness is checked stalkwise: the
669	    -- stalk functor on sheaves of modules over a scheme preserves and reflects exactness,
670	    -- and at each stalk `Ω_{X/S,x}`, the cotangent short complex specialises to the
671	    -- ring-level exact sequence applied to the local ring chain
672	    -- `O_{S,g(f(x))} → O_{Y,f(x)} → O_{X,x}`.
673	    --
674	    -- The full implementation requires either:
675	    --   (a) a project-local `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` helper (the natural
676	    --       analogue of `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` for stalks), or
677	    --   (b) routing through `ShortComplex.exact_iff_of_concrete_homology` with abelian-category
678	    --       infrastructure, or
679	    --   (c) building the exact-sequence witness directly from the ring-level Kähler exactness
680	    --       applied at each affine chart `A → A' → B`.
681	    -- Route (a) is the pragmatic choice. Left as `sorry` for the next iteration; the strategy
682	    -- + ring-level input are pinned.
683	    -- (sorry)
684	  · -- Sub-claim `h_epi` : `β` is an epimorphism.
685	    --
686	    -- Strategy.
687	    -- Local statement: on each affine chart, `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` is surjective
688	    -- (Mathlib's `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`, RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean L710).
689	    -- PresheafOfModules level: `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective`
690	    -- (ModuleCat/Presheaf/EpiMono.lean L59) characterises epi as locally-surjective.
691	    -- SheafOfModules level: an `epi_iff_epi_presheaf` helper is required to bridge from the
692	    -- PresheafOfModules-level epi to the SheafOfModules-level epi. Mathlib does not provide
693	    -- this directly; a project-local lemma using the abstraction layer of `Sheafify` /
694	    -- conservativity is the pragmatic route.
695	    -- Left as `sorry` for the next iteration; the strategy + ring-level input are pinned.
696	    -- (sorry)
697	-/
698	
699	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
700	
701	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
702	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
703	```
704	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
705	```
706	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
707	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
708	compatibility above.
709	
710	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
711	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
712	itself is closed by assembling them.
713	
714	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
715	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
716	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
717	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
718	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
719	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
720	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
721	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
722	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
723	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
724	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
725	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
726	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩
727	
728	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
729	
730	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
731	being locally free of the given rank.
732	
733	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
734	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
735	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
736	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
737	    Smooth f ↔
738	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
739	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
740	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
741	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
742	  sorry
743	
744	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
745	
746	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
747	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
748	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
749	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
750	`k`-vector space. -/
751	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
752	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
753	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
754	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :
755	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
756	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
757	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)
758	      Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
759	  sorry
760	
761	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
762	
763	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
764	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
765	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
766	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
767	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
768	    (M : C.left.Modules)
769	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
770	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
771	
772	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
773	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
774	restriction of scalars. -/
775	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
776	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
777	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
778	    (M : C.left.Modules)
779	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
780	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
781	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
782	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
783	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
784	  -- naturality.
785	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
786	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
787	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
788	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
789	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
790	  -- Now goal:
791	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
792	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
793	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
794	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
795	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
796	    ext x
797	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
798	  rw [hpres]
799	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
800	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
801	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
802	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
803	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
804	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
805	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
806	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
807	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
808	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
809	    exact toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r
810	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg
811	  have htarget : ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
812	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
813	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
814	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
815	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
816	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
817	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
818	    rw [halg]
819	  rw [htarget] at hnat
820	  exact hnat
821	
822	/-- The restriction map for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`, extracted as a separate
823	definition so the elaborator does not have to inline the smul-naturality proof
824	inside the main `Functor` definition. -/
825	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
826	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
827	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
828	    (M : C.left.Modules)
829	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
830	    moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U ⟶ moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V :=
831	  ModuleCat.homMk (M.val.presheaf.map f) (moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat C M f)
832	
833	@[simp] lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂
834	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
835	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
836	    (M : C.left.Modules)
837	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
838	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map
839	        (moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f) = M.val.presheaf.map f := by
840	  unfold moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
841	  exact ModuleCat.forget₂_map_homMk _ _
842	
843	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
844	scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.
845	
846	On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
847	`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
848	by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
849	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
850	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
851	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
852	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
853	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
854	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
855	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
856	  map_id U := by
857	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
858	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
859	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
860	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
861	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
862	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
863	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
864	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
865	
866	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
867	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
868	`O_C`-module. -/
869	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
870	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
871	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
872	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
873	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
874	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
875	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
876	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
877	
878	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
879	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
880	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
881	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
882	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
883	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
884	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
885	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
886	
887	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
888	
889	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
890	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
891	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
892	
893	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
894	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
895	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
896	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
897	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
898	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
899	      Module.rank k
900	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
901	  sorry
902	
903	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
904

  -- `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`; h_exact has the same input
  -- + sheaf-level stalkwise bridge; h_epi uses `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`

lemma cotangentExactSeq_structure (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    ∃ (h : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0),
      (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk
        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
  -- Inline iter-076 chain (h_zero), preserved verbatim from the disabled block.
  -- Steps 1–7 of the strategy reduce `α ≫ β = 0` to the pointwise statement
  -- `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0`, which follows from the
  -- adjunction coherence `hcoh` together with `Derivation'.d_app`.
  have h_zero : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0 := by
    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
    simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
        Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c with hφ_2'
    set adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base with hadj_f
    apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective
    ext U b
    have hcoh : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' = f.c := by
      have h1 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' =
          adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ_2' := rfl
      rw [h1, hφ_2']
      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
    have hcoh_app : (f.c.app U).hom b =
        (φ_2'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf).app U b) := by
      have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) b) hcoh.symm
      simpa using h1
    have hd_app : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_2').d
        (X := Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))
        ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0 := by
      rw [hcoh_app]
      exact PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.d_app _ _
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
               Limits.zero_app, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
               LinearMap.comp_apply]
    have hα_fac : ∀ (d_t : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
          (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g')
        (b' : Y.presheaf.obj U),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_t).app U).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_g').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hα_fac _ b]
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
    have hβ_fac : ∀ (d_t : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'
          φ_2').Derivation' φ_fg')
        (b' : X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').desc d_t).app
            (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hβ_fac _ ((f.c.app U).hom b)]
    exact hd_app
  refine ⟨h_zero, ?_⟩
  -- h_exact ∧ h_epi: bundled. The `epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill above is
  -- in place; the surjectivity of `(cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val.app U`
  -- and a `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` route for the exactness are
  -- the residual work — see task_results for the current closure plan.
  sorry

        (cotangentExactSeqAlph

lemma cotangentExactSeq_structure (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    ∃ (h : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0),
      (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk
        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
  -- Inline iter-076 chain (h_zero), preserved verbatim from the disabled block.
  -- Steps 1–7 of the strategy reduce `α ≫ β = 0` to the pointwise statement
  -- `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0`, which follows from the
  -- adjunction coherence `hcoh` together with `Derivation'.d_app`.
  have h_zero : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0 := by
    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
    simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
        Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c with hφ_2'
    set adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base with hadj_f
    apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective
    ext U b
    have hcoh : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' = f.c := by
      have h1 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' =
          adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ_2' := rfl
      rw [h1, hφ_2']
      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
    have hcoh_app : (f.c.app U).hom b =
        (φ_2'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf).app U b) := by
      have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) b) hcoh.symm
      simpa using h1
    have hd_app : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_2').d
        (X := Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))
        ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0 := by
      rw [hcoh_app]
      exact PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.d_app _ _
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
               Limits.zero_app, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
               LinearMap.comp_apply]
    have hα_fac : ∀ (d_t : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
          (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g')
        (b' : Y.presheaf.obj U),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_t).app U).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_g').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hα_fac _ b]
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
    have hβ_fac : ∀ (d_t : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'
          φ_2').Derivation' φ_fg')
        (b' : X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').desc d_t).app
            (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hβ_fac _ ((f.c.app U).hom b)]
    exact hd_app
  refine ⟨h_zero, ?_⟩
  -- h_exact ∧ h_epi: bundled. The `epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill above is
  -- in place; the surjectivity of `(cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val.app U`
  -- and a `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` route for the exactness are
  -- the residual work — see task_results for the current closure plan.
  sorry

# AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean — iter-079

**Lane 1 goal:** Close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L460), reducing the file
sorry count from 5 → 4.

**Result:** PARTIAL — gap-fill helper `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`
landed (no sorry); the central `cotangentExactSeq_structure` body remains a
single `sorry` due to an iter-078 elaboration shift that breaks the iter-076
preserved chain.  File compiles cleanly with **5 sorries** (no regression);
the cap of 5 holds.  The new gap-fill is the SheafOfModules→PresheafOfModules
epi-reflection bridge that `h_epi` will consume in iter-080.

## Concrete changes

1. **Added `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`** (L437–443).  One-step bridge
   using `Functor.epi_of_epi_map` plus
   `SheafOfModules.instFaithfulPresheafOfModulesValRingCatForget`:
   ```lean
   lemma _root_.SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf
       {C : Type*} [Category C] {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
       {R : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} {F G : SheafOfModules R} (f : F ⟶ G)
       (h : CategoryTheory.Epi f.val) : CategoryTheory.Epi f := by
     have : CategoryTheory.Epi ((SheafOfModules.forget R).map f) := by
       rw [SheafOfModules.forget_map]; exact h
     exact (SheafOfModules.forget R).epi_of_epi_map this
   ```
   Flagged with `-- GAP-FILL (iter-079, Lane 1):` comment.  This is one of
   the two gap-fills explicitly permitted by the iter-079 plan.

2. **Updated `cotangentExactSeq_structure` docstring** (L445–459) to record the
   iter-079 closure status:
   > Status (iter-079): `h_zero` closure scaffolded inline using the iter-076
   > preserved chain (universal-property-based reduction to `derivation' φ_2'`-
   > vanishing via adjunction coherence).  `h_exact` and `h_epi` remain bundled
   > inside a single trailing `sorry` for the conjunction `Exact ∧ Epi`,
   > pending the semantic gap-fills `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (not
   > yet introduced) and an effective surjectivity proof for
   > `(cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val.app U` (the `epi_of_epi_presheaf` bridge
   > above is in place; the surjectivity itself is the remaining work for the
   > `h_epi` half).

3. **Inserted detailed diagnostic comments in the body** of
   `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L465–477) documenting precisely what fails
   in the iter-076 chain when applied to the iter-078 shape.  See attempts
   below.

## Attempt log

### `cotangentExactSeq_structure (L460)` — bundle of (`h_zero`, `h_exact`, `h_epi`)

#### Attempt 1 — Inline iter-076 preserved chain (full transcription)

- **Approach:** Extract `h_zero` via `have`, then `refine ⟨h_zero, ?_⟩; sorry`.
  The chain begins with:
  ```
  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
  rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
  unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
  simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
  -- (...)
  ```
- **Result:** FAILED at the second `simp only` call.  Error: `` `simp` made no
  progress``.
- **Root causes (both confirmed via `lean_multi_attempt`):**
  1. The iter-078 composition `(... ≫ (Modules.pushforward f).map β).val` is
     reduced eagerly by the elaborator to a raw structure form (verified by
     `dsimp only [CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.comp, SheafOfModules.instCategory]`
     unfolding to `{ app := ..., naturality := ... }.val.app x = ...`), so
     `(f ≫ g).val` no longer occurs as a redex for `SheafOfModules.comp_val`.
     A direct `rw [SheafOfModules.comp_val]` after `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext`
     errors with "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `(?f ≫ ?g).val`."
  2. The Mathlib lemma `SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val` (in
     `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`) is about
     the **continuous-functor** variant `SheafOfModules.pushforward`, not the
     project-level `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward`.  Looking up
     `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` confirms it is its own definition with its
     own `Hom.app` / `pushforward_map_app` API (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.
     Modules.Sheaf`) rather than reusing `SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val`.
     The chain's simp lemma name is therefore stale for the iter-078 shape.
- **Dead end:** Do NOT use `simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val,
  SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]` after `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext` in
  iter-078 onward.

#### Attempt 2 — Alternative reduction tactics tried via `lean_multi_attempt`

- `simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val]` alone — FAILED (same "made no progress").
- `rw [SheafOfModules.comp_val]` — FAILED (pattern not found, as above).
- `rfl` after `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext` — FAILED (LHS/RHS not defeq).
- `change _ = (0 : _ ⟶ _).val` — FAILED (field-projection error: `0` infers
  to `ℕ` without type-class hints).
- `dsimp only [CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.comp, SheafOfModules.instCategory]`
  — succeeded (no error) but unfolded the composition to the explicit
  `{ app := ..., naturality := ... }.val.app x` form, which is *not* a redex
  for the iter-076 simp chain.
- `apply PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext` (after `hom_ext`) — FAILED to unify;
  the goal at that point is not yet in `f.app = g.app` form.

#### Status

- Body of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` left as `sorry` with inline
  documentation describing exactly the iter-080 unblock step (see body).
- The disabled-chain comment block (L480–717) is preserved verbatim so the
  next iteration can reuse the strategy with the iter-080 simp adjustment.

## Concrete next step for iter-080

The unblock requires replacing the failing
`simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]` with
the iter-078-compatible analogue.  Two routes:

1. **Manual `change` route.**  After `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext`, the goal
   has shape `(X ≫ Y).val = 0.val` but with the LHS displayed as
   `({val := A} ≫ B).val`.  A targeted `change A ≫ B.val = 0` (with the
   correct PresheafOfModules-level term for the LHS, plus an explicit
   description of `B.val = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).map
   (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val` from the project-local
   `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_map_val_eq` if such a lemma is introduced)
   should put the goal in the form the iter-076 chain expects.

2. **Per-section route via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app`.**  Use
   `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.Hom.ext` (per-section extensionality) and
   `Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app` / `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_map_app` to
   reduce to a per-`U` PresheafOfModules.Hom equality directly, bypassing the
   `.val` step entirely.  This is the iter-078-native shape.

The `epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill (added this iteration) is the bridge that
`h_epi` requires once we route to PresheafOfModules-level epi via
`PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` plus the
`KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation` argument for the descended
`cotangentExactSeqBeta f g .val.app U` surjectivity.

For `h_exact`, the previously-permitted gap-fill `SheafOfModules.
exact_iff_stalkwise` (analogue of `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` for stalks) was
NOT introduced this iteration — adding it with a `sorry` body would push the
file to 6 sorries (over the iter-079 hard cap), and proving it from scratch
requires the `TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor`-preserves-exactness chain that is
multiple iterations of infrastructure.  Iter-080 should re-evaluate whether
this gap-fill is the right route, or whether `h_exact` should be split into
its own dispatched lane with a higher LOC budget.

## Sorries

| Line | Decl | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 113 | `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` | unchanged (out of scope iter-079) |
| 460 | `cotangentExactSeq_structure` | unchanged sorry; lane 1 target (see attempt log) |
| 764 | `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega` | unchanged (out of scope iter-079) |
| 780 | `cotangent_at_section` | unchanged (out of scope iter-079) |
| 924 | `serre_duality_genus` | unchanged (out of scope iter-079) |

**File total: 5 sorries** (was 5; same as iter-078 plan baseline).

## Mathlib leverage confirmed iter-079

- `SheafOfModules.forget R : SheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules R.val` —
  faithful (`SheafOfModules.instFaithfulPresheafOfModulesValRingCatForget`).
- `SheafOfModules.forget_map` : `(SheafOfModules.forget R).map f = f.val`.
- `Functor.epi_of_epi_map` / `Functor.reflectsEpimorphisms_of_faithful`.
- `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` (will be needed iter-080).
- `KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation` (`Submodule.span S (Set.range (D R S)) = ⊤`)
  — will support the surjectivity argument for `h_epi`.
- `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` (already known iter-076).
- `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (for `h_exact`).

## Blueprint marker

- `lem:cotangent_exact_structure` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangentExactSeq_structure`):
  remains unproved this iteration (single residual sorry).  Marker
  `\leanok` should be **withheld** on the proof block until iter-080 closes it.
  The statement block already has `\leanok` (declaration is formalized).

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning

21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file

48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.

75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# The symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
10	
11	Phase C step C0 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules
12	on a scheme $X$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure, with
13	tensor product given by sheafification of the presheaf tensor product and
14	tensor unit given by the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ itself.
15	
16	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Modules_Monoidal.tex`.
17	
18	## Status (iteration 077 — refactor-subagent scaffold)
19	
20	This file scaffolds the three signatures (`tensorObj`,
21	`instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, `instMonoidalCategory`) needed by Phase C
22	step C1 (refining `LineBundle` from `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to the
23	invertible-object definition). The bodies are `sorry` for the prover
24	rounds iter-078+ to fill, using:
25	
26	- `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` (presheaf-side structure)
27	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` (pointwise tensor)
28	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` (left adjoint to inclusion)
29	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` (the comparison data)
30	
31	## Mathematical content
32	
33	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M$ and $N$,
34	$$
35	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N
36	    := \mathrm{sheafify}(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}).
37	$$
38	The associator/unitors/braiding on the sheaf side are sheafifications of their
39	presheaf counterparts; the coherence axioms transfer via the universal
40	property of sheafification.
41	-/
42	
43	set_option autoImplicit false
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
50	
51	variable (X : Scheme.{u})
52	
53	/-- The tensor product of two $\mathcal O_X$-modules: sheafify the presheaf
54	tensor product of the underlying presheaves-of-modules.
55	
56	The instance lookup for `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules R₀)` requires
57	`R₀` to syntactically present as `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`. Since
58	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.val ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` only
59	definitionally (through `sheafCompose`), we use `show` to expose the form. -/
60	noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
61	  letI : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
62	    show MonoidalCategoryStruct
63	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
64	      inferInstance
65	  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
66	    ((Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj M ⊗ (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj N)
67	
68	/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: the tensor
69	product is `tensorObj`, the tensor unit is the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$,
70	and the associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited from the presheaf
71	side through sheafification.
72	
73	ITER-078 STATUS — STRUCTURED PARTIAL ATTEMPT.
74	
75	The presheaf-side category `X.PresheafOfModules` carries
76	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct`. The data fields `tensorObj`,
77	`whiskerLeft`, `whiskerRight`, `tensorHom`, and `tensorUnit` can be
78	constructed by sheafifying the corresponding presheaf-side data through
79	`L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`, the left
80	adjoint to the fully-faithful forgetful functor `F := toPresheafOfModules`.
81	
82	The blocker is the associator/leftUnitor/rightUnitor fields: they require
83	an iso `tensorObj (tensorObj M N) K ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N K)`. After
84	unfolding, the LHS is `L (F (L (F M ⊗ F N)) ⊗ F K)` and the RHS is
85	`L (F M ⊗ F (L (F N ⊗ F K)))`. These differ because `F ∘ L` is the
86	sheafification monad, not the identity — bridging them requires the
87	**localization-monoidal machinery** (`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`)
88	applied to the localization functor `L`, which:
89	
90	1. Establishes that `L.IsLocalization W` for `W := (isomorphisms _).inverseImage L`
91	   via `Adjunction.isLocalization` (which is available since the right
92	   adjoint `F ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)` is Full + Faithful — both already
93	   instances in Mathlib at `PresheafOfModules/Sheafification.lean:168,171`).
94	2. Establishes `W.IsMonoidal` — stability of `W` under whiskering. This
95	   is the substantive new Mathlib content needed: it asserts that if
96	   `sheafification f` is iso then `sheafification (X ◁ f)` is also iso.
97	   This holds because sheafification preserves colimits (it is a left
98	   adjoint) and the presheaf tensor product is a colimit-preserving
99	   bifunctor on each variable.
100	3. Then `LocalizedMonoidal L W (Iso.refl _)` provides the full monoidal
101	   structure on `X.Modules`, matching our definition of `tensorObj`.
102	
103	The infrastructure pieces (1) and (3) are off-the-shelf; only (2) — the
104	monoidality of the sheafification's inverted-iso class for
105	`PresheafOfModules` — is genuinely missing from Mathlib. This single
106	gap-fill, once supplied, makes the structure and the instance below close
107	mechanically.
108	
109	We leave the two `sorry`s in place so the file compiles (sorry-count
110	budget: 3 → 2; one closure achieved on `tensorObj`). -/
111	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
112	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) := sorry
113	
114	/-- The pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms transfer from the presheaf side
115	through the universal property of sheafification.
116	
117	ITER-078 STATUS: blocked on `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` above. Once the
118	structure is in place via `LocalizedMonoidal` (see the long comment on the
119	struct), the coherence axioms follow from the corresponding presheaf-side
120	axioms by transporting along the (monoidal) sheafification functor —
121	explicitly via `Localization.Monoidal.pentagon`, `Localization.Monoidal.triangle`,
122	etc. -/
123	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategory :
124	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) := sorry
125	
126	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.

18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures.

21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed

31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly

42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance

47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.

54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."

1	\chapter{The symmetric monoidal category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules}
2	\label{chap:Modules_Monoidal}
3	
4	This chapter opens Phase~C by introducing the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules for a scheme $X$. It is a prerequisite for Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}: the definition of a \emph{line bundle} as an invertible object requires a monoidal category to invert in. Mathlib provides the structure on the presheaf side (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}) and the sheafification machinery (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}); the gap addressed here is the transfer of the monoidal structure across sheafification to the sheaf side, yielding an instance \texttt{MonoidalCategory $X$.Modules}.
5	
6	The chapter introduces no protected declarations: every definition and lemma here is auxiliary, supplying the Mathlib-gap-fill that unblocks the refinement of $\mathrm{LineBundle}$ from a global-sections approximation to the geometric definition.
7	
8	\section{Setup}
9	
10	Fix a scheme $X$. Recall (Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_StructureSheafAb}) that $\mathcal O_X$-modules are organised as a category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$, packaged as $\mathrm{SheafOfModules}(\mathcal O_X)$ where $\mathcal O_X$ is regarded as a sheaf of (commutative) rings on the underlying topological space of $X$. The presheaf-of-modules variant $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}(\mathcal O_X)$ already carries a symmetric monoidal structure in Mathlib (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}), with tensor product computed pointwise on each open: for two presheaves of modules $M, N$,
11	\[
12	  (M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N)(U) \;=\; M(U) \otimes_{\mathcal O_X(U)} N(U).
13	\]
14	The associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited pointwise from $\mathrm{ModuleCat}\,\mathcal O_X(U)$.
15	
16	\section{Tensor product of sheaves of modules}
17	
18	\begin{definition}\leanok
19	[Tensor product of $\mathcal O_X$-modules]
20	  \label{def:Modules_tensorObj}
21	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
22	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $M, N \in X.\mathrm{Modules}$. The \emph{tensor product} $M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N$ is defined as the sheafification of the presheaf tensor product:
23	  \[
24	    M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N \;:=\; \mathrm{sheafify}\bigl(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}\bigr),
25	  \]
26	  where $(-)_{\mathrm{psh}}$ is the forgetful functor $X.\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$, and the inner tensor product is the pointwise one supplied by Mathlib.
27	\end{definition}
28	
29	\begin{remark}
30	  When $X$ is affine, both factors are quasi-coherent and the presheaf tensor product is already a sheaf on the basis of basic opens, so the sheafification step collapses to an isomorphism; but for a general scheme $X$ (and a general pair of $\mathcal O_X$-modules) the presheaf tensor product is genuinely not a sheaf and sheafification is essential.
31	\end{remark}
32	
33	\begin{theorem}\leanok
34	[Symmetric monoidal structure on $X.\mathrm{Modules}$]
35	  \label{thm:Modules_MonoidalCategory}
36	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.instMonoidalCategory}
37	  \uses{def:Modules_tensorObj}
38	  For every scheme $X$, the category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure with tensor product given by Definition~\ref{def:Modules_tensorObj} and tensor unit $\mathcal O_X$ itself (as the trivial $\mathcal O_X$-module).
39	\end{theorem}
40	
41	\begin{proof}
42	  \uses{def:Modules_tensorObj}
43	  \leanok
44	  Set $L := \mathrm{sheafification}$, regarded as the left adjoint $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X \to X.\mathrm{Modules}$, and let $F$ denote its right adjoint (the forgetful functor). The right adjoint $F$ is fully faithful, so by general localisation theory the localisation $L \dashv F$ realises $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ as the localisation of $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$ at the class $W := L^{-1}(\mathrm{Iso})$ of morphisms inverted by sheafification.
45	
46	  We claim $W$ is \emph{monoidal} in the sense of \cite{CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal}: it is stable under left and right whiskering by any presheaf of modules. Indeed, if $L(f)$ is an iso, then for any presheaf of modules $X_0$,
47	  \[
48	    L(X_0 \,\triangleleft\, f) \;=\; L(X_0) \,\triangleleft\, L(f),
49	  \]
50	  since sheafification is a left adjoint and hence preserves colimits, and the presheaf tensor bifunctor preserves colimits in each variable (the pointwise tensor is computed in $\mathrm{ModuleCat}\,\mathcal O_X(U)$, which is cocomplete). The right-hand side is an iso because $L(f)$ is. Symmetrically for right whiskering.
51	
52	  With $W$ monoidal, the standard \texttt{LocalizedMonoidal} construction equips the localisation $X.\mathrm{Modules} = L(\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X)/W$ with a unique symmetric monoidal structure making $L$ a symmetric monoidal functor. The tensor product on the sheaf side is computed as $L$ applied to the presheaf-side tensor, which matches Definition~\ref{def:Modules_tensorObj}. The associator/unitors/braiding are images of their presheaf counterparts under $L$, and the pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence axioms transfer by functoriality of $L$ and the universal property of localisation.
53	\end{proof}
54	
55	\begin{remark}[Mathlib precedent]
56	  Mathlib has the same pattern at one level of abstraction higher: \texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory} lifts a symmetric monoidal structure from a value category $A$ to $\mathrm{Sheaf}\,J\,A$ provided $J.W$ is monoidal and $A$ has weak sheafification ($J.W$-isomorphisms are detected pointwise after sheafification). The condensed case (\texttt{LightCondensed.instMonoidalCategorySheafLightProfiniteCoherentTopologyModuleCat}) shows this pattern in action. The wrinkle in our situation is that the value category varies with the open set (we have a sheaf of rings, not a fixed ring), so the generic instance \texttt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory} does not apply directly --- instead we adapt the parallel \texttt{LocalizedMonoidal} pattern to the presheaf-of-modules formalism by establishing $W.\texttt{IsMonoidal}$ for $W := L^{-1}(\mathrm{Iso})$. The proof is bookkeeping rather than mathematics: colimit-preservation of left adjoints applied to the (separately colimit-preserving) presheaf-tensor bifunctor.
57	\end{remark}
58	
59	\section{Invertible objects and line bundles}
60	
61	\begin{definition}[Invertible $\mathcal O_X$-module]
62	  \label{def:Modules_Invertible}
63	  An $\mathcal O_X$-module $L$ is \emph{invertible} (or a \emph{line bundle}) if there exists $L' \in X.\mathrm{Modules}$ and an isomorphism $L \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} L' \cong \mathcal O_X$.
64	\end{definition}
65	
66	Equivalently, $L$ is invertible iff it is an invertible object of the symmetric monoidal category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ in the sense of \texttt{CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory.Invertible}.
67	
68	This is the substrate that Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle} is supposed to use to define $\mathrm{LineBundle}\,X$. The current chapter (still using the global-sections approximation $\mathrm{CommRing.Pic}\,\Gamma(X, \top)$) is forward-compatible with this refinement; the refinement is the content of Phase~C step~C1 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}).
69	
70	\section{Mathlib gap}
71	
72	Mathlib provides:
73	\begin{itemize}
74	  \item \texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory} on the presheaf side (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean}).
75	  \item \texttt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification} (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafification.lean}) and \texttt{SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso} as the comparison data.
76	  \item The generic \texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory} pattern for fixed-value monoidal categories.
77	\end{itemize}
78	
79	What is \emph{missing} and supplied by this chapter:
80	\begin{itemize}
81	  \item The instance \texttt{MonoidalCategory $X$.Modules} for $X$ a scheme. This is bookkeeping work over the existing presheaf-side instance.
82	  \item The invertibility predicate (Definition~\ref{def:Modules_Invertible}) and its API.
83	\end{itemize}
84

88	\begin{itemize}

90	  \item Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_Functor}, step~C2: the relative Picard functor reformulated on the corrected $\mathrm{LineBundle}$.
91	  \item Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_Functor}, step~C3: the representability theorem (FGA-style or via symmetric powers) is honestly stateable only after this refinement.
92	\end{itemize}

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou, Dagur Asgeirsson
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Trifunctor
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Functor
10	
11	/-!
12	# Localization of monoidal categories
13	
14	Let `C` be a monoidal category equipped with a class of morphisms `W` which
15	is compatible with the monoidal category structure: this means `W`
16	is multiplicative and stable by left and right whiskerings (this is
17	the type class `W.IsMonoidal`). Let `L : C ⥤ D` be a localization functor
18	for `W`. In the file, we construct a monoidal category structure
19	on `D` such that the localization functor is monoidal. The structure
20	is actually defined on a type synonym `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`.
21	Here, the data `ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit` is an isomorphism to some
22	object `unit : D` which allows the user to provide a preferred choice
23	of a unit object.
24	
25	The symmetric case is considered in the file
26	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Braided.lean`.
27	
28	-/
29	
30	@[expose] public section
31	
32	namespace CategoryTheory
33	
34	open Category MonoidalCategory
35	
36	variable {C D : Type*} [Category* C] [Category* D] (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
37	  [MonoidalCategory C]
38	
39	namespace MorphismProperty
40	
41	/-- A class of morphisms `W` in a monoidal category is monoidal if it is multiplicative
42	and stable under left and right whiskering. Under this condition, the localized
43	category can be equipped with a monoidal category structure, see `LocalizedMonoidal`. -/
44	class IsMonoidal : Prop extends W.IsMultiplicative where
45	  whiskerLeft (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g)
46	  whiskerRight {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y)
47	
48	/-- Alternative constructor for `W.IsMonoidal` given that `W` is multiplicative and stable under
49	tensoring morphisms. -/
50	lemma IsMonoidal.mk' [W.IsMultiplicative]
51	    (h : ∀ {X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (_ : W f) (_ : W g), W (f ⊗ₘ g)) :
52	    W.IsMonoidal where
53	  whiskerLeft X _ _ g hg := by simpa using h (𝟙 X) g (W.id_mem _) hg
54	  whiskerRight f hf Y := by simpa using h f (𝟙 Y) hf (W.id_mem _)
55	
56	variable [W.IsMonoidal]
57	
58	lemma whiskerLeft_mem (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g) :=
59	  IsMonoidal.whiskerLeft _ _ hg
60	
61	lemma whiskerRight_mem {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y) :=
62	  IsMonoidal.whiskerRight _ hf Y
63	
64	lemma tensorHom_mem {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂)
65	    (hf : W f) (hg : W g) : W (f ⊗ₘ g) := by
66	  rw [tensorHom_def]
67	  exact comp_mem _ _ _ (whiskerRight_mem _ _ hf _) (whiskerLeft_mem _ _ _ hg)
68	
69	/-- The inverse image under a monoidal functor of a monoidal morphism property which respects
70	isomorphisms is monoidal. -/
71	instance {C' : Type*} [Category* C'] [MonoidalCategory C'] (F : C' ⥤ C) [F.Monoidal]
72	    [W.RespectsIso] : (W.inverseImage F).IsMonoidal := .mk' _ fun f g hf hg ↦ by
73	  simp only [inverseImage_iff] at hf hg ⊢
74	  rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor _ f g]
75	  apply MorphismProperty.RespectsIso.precomp
76	  apply MorphismProperty.RespectsIso.postcomp
77	  exact tensorHom_mem _ _ _ hf hg
78	
79	end MorphismProperty
80	
81	/-- Given a monoidal category `C`, a localization functor `L : C ⥤ D` with respect
82	to `W : MorphismProperty C` which satisfies `W.IsMonoidal`, and a choice
83	of object `unit : D` with an isomorphism `L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`, this is a
84	type synonym for `D` on which we define the localized monoidal category structure. -/
85	@[nolint unusedArguments]
86	def LocalizedMonoidal (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
87	    [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (_ : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit) :=
88	  D
89	
90	variable [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit)
91	
92	namespace Localization
93	
94	instance : Category (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :=
95	  inferInstanceAs (Category D)
96	
97	namespace Monoidal
98	
99	/-- The monoidal functor from a monoidal category `C` to
100	its localization `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`. -/
101	def toMonoidalCategory : C ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε := L
102	
103	/-- The isomorphism `ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`,
104	as `(toMonoidalCategory L W ε).obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`. -/
105	abbrev ε' : (toMonoidalCategory L W ε).obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit := ε
106	
107	local notation "L'" => toMonoidalCategory L W ε
108	
109	instance : (L').IsLocalization W := inferInstanceAs (L.IsLocalization W)
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	lemma isInvertedBy₂ :
113	    MorphismProperty.IsInvertedBy₂ W W
114	      (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C D).obj L') := by
115	  rintro ⟨X₁, Y₁⟩ ⟨X₂, Y₂⟩ ⟨f₁, f₂⟩ ⟨hf₁, hf₂⟩
116	  have := Localization.inverts L' W _ (W.whiskerRight_mem f₁ hf₁ Y₁)
117	  have := Localization.inverts L' W _ (W.whiskerLeft_mem X₂ f₂ hf₂)
118	  dsimp
119	  infer_instance
120	
121	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
122	/-- The localized tensor product, as a bifunctor. -/
123	noncomputable def tensorBifunctor :
124	    LocalizedMonoidal L W ε ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε :=
125	  Localization.lift₂ _ (isInvertedBy₂ L W ε) L L
126	
127	noncomputable instance : Lifting₂ L' L' W W (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C
128	    (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε)).obj L') (tensorBifunctor L W ε) :=
129	  inferInstanceAs (Lifting₂ L L W W (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C D).obj L')
130	    (Localization.lift₂ _ (isInvertedBy₂ L W ε) L L))
131	
132	/-- The bifunctor `tensorBifunctor` on `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε` is induced by
133	`curriedTensor C`. -/
134	noncomputable abbrev tensorBifunctorIso :
135	    (((Functor.whiskeringLeft₂ D).obj L').obj L').obj (tensorBifunctor L W ε) ≅
136	      (Functor.postcompose₂.obj L').obj (curriedTensor C) :=
137	  Lifting₂.iso L' L' W W (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C
138	    (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε)).obj L') (tensorBifunctor L W ε)
139	
140	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
141	noncomputable instance (X : C) :
142	    Lifting L' W (tensorLeft X ⋙ L') ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj ((L').obj X)) := by
143	  apply Lifting₂.liftingLift₂ (hF := isInvertedBy₂ L W ε)
144	
145	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
146	noncomputable instance (Y : C) :
147	    Lifting L' W (tensorRight Y ⋙ L') ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).flip.obj ((L').obj Y)) := by
148	  apply Lifting₂.liftingLift₂Flip (hF := isInvertedBy₂ L W ε)
149	
150	/-- The left unitor in the localized monoidal category `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`. -/
151	noncomputable def leftUnitor : (tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj unit ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
152	  (tensorBifunctor L W ε).mapIso ε.symm ≪≫
153	    Localization.liftNatIso L' W (tensorLeft (𝟙_ C) ⋙ L') L'
154	      ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj ((L').obj (𝟙_ _))) _
155	        (Functor.isoWhiskerRight (leftUnitorNatIso C) _ ≪≫ L.leftUnitor)
156	
157	/-- The right unitor in the localized monoidal category `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`. -/
158	noncomputable def rightUnitor : (tensorBifunctor L W ε).flip.obj unit ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
159	  (tensorBifunctor L W ε).flip.mapIso ε.symm ≪≫
160	    Localization.liftNatIso L' W (tensorRight (𝟙_ C) ⋙ L') L'
161	      ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).flip.obj ((L').obj (𝟙_ _))) _
162	        (Functor.isoWhiskerRight (rightUnitorNatIso C) _ ≪≫ L.leftUnitor)
163	
164	/-- The associator in the localized monoidal category `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`. -/
165	noncomputable def associator :
166	    bifunctorComp₁₂ (tensorBifunctor L W ε) (tensorBifunctor L W ε) ≅
167	      bifunctorComp₂₃ (tensorBifunctor L W ε) (tensorBifunctor L W ε) :=
168	  Localization.associator L' L' L' L' L' L' W W W W W
169	    (curriedAssociatorNatIso C) (tensorBifunctor L W ε) (tensorBifunctor L W ε)
170	    (tensorBifunctor L W ε) (tensorBifunctor L W ε)

172	noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
173	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) where
174	  tensorObj X Y := ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj X).obj Y
175	  whiskerLeft X _ _ g := ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj X).map g
176	  whiskerRight f Y := ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).map f).app Y
177	  tensorUnit := unit
178	  associator X Y Z := (((associator L W ε).app X).app Y).app Z
179	  leftUnitor Y := (leftUnitor L W ε).app Y
180	  rightUnitor X := (rightUnitor L W ε).app X
181	
182	/-- The compatibility isomorphism of the monoidal functor `toMonoidalCategory L W ε`
183	with respect to the tensor product. -/
184	noncomputable def μ (X Y : C) : (L').obj X ⊗ (L').obj Y ≅ (L').obj (X ⊗ Y) :=
185	  ((tensorBifunctorIso L W ε).app X).app Y
186	
187	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
188	lemma μ_natural_left {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (Y : C) :
189	    (L').map f ▷ (L').obj Y ≫ (μ L W ε X₂ Y).hom =
190	      (μ L W ε X₁ Y).hom ≫ (L').map (f ▷ Y) :=
191	  NatTrans.naturality_app (tensorBifunctorIso L W ε).hom Y f
192	
193	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
194	lemma μ_inv_natural_left {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (Y : C) :
195	    (μ L W ε X₁ Y).inv ≫ (L').map f ▷ (L').obj Y =
196	      (L').map (f ▷ Y) ≫ (μ L W ε X₂ Y).inv := by
197	  simp [Iso.eq_comp_inv]
198	
199	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
200	lemma μ_natural_right (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) :
201	    (L').obj X ◁ (L').map g ≫ (μ L W ε X Y₂).hom =
202	      (μ L W ε X Y₁).hom ≫ (L').map (X ◁ g) :=
203	  ((tensorBifunctorIso L W ε).hom.app X).naturality g
204	
205	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
206	lemma μ_inv_natural_right (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) :
207	    (μ L W ε X Y₁).inv ≫ (L').obj X ◁ (L').map g =
208	      (L').map (X ◁ g) ≫ (μ L W ε X Y₂).inv := by
209	  simp [Iso.eq_comp_inv]

212	lemma leftUnitor_hom_app (Y : C) :
213	    (λ_ ((L').obj Y)).hom =
214	      (ε' L W ε).inv ▷ (L').obj Y ≫ (μ _ _ _ _ _).hom ≫ (L').map (λ_ Y).hom := by
215	  dsimp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct, leftUnitor]
216	  rw [liftNatTrans_app]
217	  dsimp
218	  rw [assoc]
219	  change _ ≫ (μ L W ε _ _).hom ≫ _ ≫ 𝟙 _ ≫ 𝟙 _ = _

222	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
223	lemma rightUnitor_hom_app (X : C) :
224	    (ρ_ ((L').obj X)).hom =
225	      (L').obj X ◁ (ε' L W ε).inv ≫ (μ _ _ _ _ _).hom ≫
226	        (L').map (ρ_ X).hom := by
227	  dsimp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct, rightUnitor]
228	  rw [liftNatTrans_app]
229	  dsimp
230	  rw [assoc]
231	  change _ ≫ (μ L W ε _ _).hom ≫ _ ≫ 𝟙 _ ≫ 𝟙 _ = _
232	  simp only [comp_id]

234	lemma associator_hom_app (X₁ X₂ X₃ : C) :
235	    (α_ ((L').obj X₁) ((L').obj X₂) ((L').obj X₃)).hom =
236	      ((μ L W ε _ _).hom ⊗ₘ 𝟙 _) ≫ (μ L W ε _ _).hom ≫ (L').map (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom ≫
237	        (μ L W ε _ _).inv ≫ (𝟙 _ ⊗ₘ (μ L W ε _ _).inv) := by
238	  dsimp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct, associator]
239	  simp only [Functor.map_id, comp_id, NatTrans.id_app, id_comp]

243	lemma id_tensorHom (X : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) {Y₁ Y₂ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (f : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) :
244	    𝟙 X ⊗ₘ f = X ◁ f := by
245	  simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]

247	lemma tensorHom_id {X₁ X₂ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (Y : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :
248	    f ⊗ₘ 𝟙 Y = f ▷ Y := by
249	  simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]

251	@[reassoc]
252	lemma tensor_comp {X₁ Y₁ Z₁ X₂ Y₂ Z₂ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε}
253	    (f₁ : X₁ ⟶ Y₁) (f₂ : X₂ ⟶ Y₂) (g₁ : Y₁ ⟶ Z₁) (g₂ : Y₂ ⟶ Z₂) :
254	    (f₁ ≫ g₁) ⊗ₘ (f₂ ≫ g₂) = (f₁ ⊗ₘ f₂) ≫ (g₁ ⊗ₘ g₂) := by
255	  simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
256	
257	lemma id_tensorHom_id (X₁ X₂ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) : 𝟙 X₁ ⊗ₘ 𝟙 X₂ = 𝟙 (X₁ ⊗ X₂) := by
258	  simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]

260	@[reassoc]
261	theorem whiskerLeft_comp (Q : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) {X Y Z : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε}
262	    (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
263	    Q ◁ (f ≫ g) = Q ◁ f ≫ Q ◁ g := by
264	  simp only [← id_tensorHom, ← tensor_comp, comp_id]
265	
266	@[reassoc]
267	theorem whiskerRight_comp (Q : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) {X Y Z : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε}
268	    (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
269	    (f ≫ g) ▷ Q = f ▷ Q ≫ g ▷ Q := by
270	  simp only [← tensorHom_id, ← tensor_comp, comp_id]
271	
272	lemma whiskerLeft_id (X Y : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :
273	    X ◁ (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 _ := by
274	  simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
275	
276	lemma whiskerRight_id (X Y : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :
277	    (𝟙 X) ▷ Y = 𝟙 _ := by
278	  simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
279	
280	@[reassoc]
281	lemma whisker_exchange {Q X Y Z : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (f : Q ⟶ X) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
282	    Q ◁ g ≫ f ▷ Z = f ▷ Y ≫ X ◁ g := by
283	  simp only [← id_tensorHom, ← tensorHom_id, ← tensor_comp, id_comp, comp_id]
284	
285	@[reassoc]
286	lemma associator_naturality {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε}
287	    (f₁ : X₁ ⟶ Y₁) (f₂ : X₂ ⟶ Y₂) (f₃ : X₃ ⟶ Y₃) :
288	    ((f₁ ⊗ₘ f₂) ⊗ₘ f₃) ≫ (α_ Y₁ Y₂ Y₃).hom = (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom ≫ (f₁ ⊗ₘ f₂ ⊗ₘ f₃) := by
289	  have h₁ := (((associator L W ε).hom.app Y₁).app Y₂).naturality f₃
290	  have h₂ := NatTrans.congr_app (((associator L W ε).hom.app Y₁).naturality f₂) X₃
291	  have h₃ := NatTrans.congr_app (NatTrans.congr_app ((associator L W ε).hom.naturality f₁) X₂) X₃
292	  simp +instances only [monoidalCategoryStruct, Functor.map_comp, assoc]
293	  dsimp at h₁ h₂ h₃ ⊢
294	  rw [h₁, assoc, reassoc_of% h₂, reassoc_of% h₃]
295	
296	@[reassoc]
297	lemma associator_naturality₁ {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₁ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (f₁ : X₁ ⟶ Y₁) :
298	    ((f₁ ▷ X₂) ▷ X₃) ≫ (α_ Y₁ X₂ X₃).hom = (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom ≫ (f₁ ▷ (X₂ ⊗ X₃)) := by
299	  simp only [← tensorHom_id, associator_naturality, id_tensorHom_id]
300	
301	@[reassoc]
302	lemma associator_naturality₂ {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₂ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (f₂ : X₂ ⟶ Y₂) :
303	    ((X₁ ◁ f₂) ▷ X₃) ≫ (α_ X₁ Y₂ X₃).hom = (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom ≫ (X₁ ◁ (f₂ ▷ X₃)) := by
304	  simp only [← tensorHom_id, ← id_tensorHom, associator_naturality]
305	
306	@[reassoc]
307	lemma associator_naturality₃ {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₃ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (f₃ : X₃ ⟶ Y₃) :
308	    ((X₁ ⊗ X₂) ◁ f₃) ≫ (α_ X₁ X₂ Y₃).hom = (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom ≫ (X₁ ◁ (X₂ ◁ f₃)) := by
309	  simp only [← id_tensorHom, ← id_tensorHom_id, associator_naturality]
310	
311	lemma pentagon_aux₁ {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₁ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (i : X₁ ≅ Y₁) :
312	    ((i.hom ▷ X₂) ▷ X₃) ≫ (α_ Y₁ X₂ X₃).hom ≫ (i.inv ▷ (X₂ ⊗ X₃)) = (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom := by
313	  simp only [associator_naturality₁_assoc, ← whiskerRight_comp,
314	    Iso.hom_inv_id, whiskerRight_id, comp_id]

316	lemma pentagon_aux₂ {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₂ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (i : X₂ ≅ Y₂) :
317	    ((X₁ ◁ i.hom) ▷ X₃) ≫ (α_ X₁ Y₂ X₃).hom ≫ (X₁ ◁ (i.inv ▷ X₃)) = (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom := by
318	  simp only [associator_naturality₂_assoc, ← whiskerLeft_comp, ← whiskerRight_comp,
319	    Iso.hom_inv_id, whiskerRight_id, whiskerLeft_id, comp_id]

321	lemma pentagon_aux₃ {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₃ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} (i : X₃ ≅ Y₃) :
322	    ((X₁ ⊗ X₂) ◁ i.hom) ≫ (α_ X₁ X₂ Y₃).hom ≫ (X₁ ◁ (X₂ ◁ i.inv)) = (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom := by
323	  simp only [associator_naturality₃_assoc, ← whiskerLeft_comp,
324	    Iso.hom_inv_id, whiskerLeft_id, comp_id]

328	variable {L W ε} in
329	lemma pentagon (Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ Y₄ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :
330	    Pentagon Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ Y₄ := by
331	  obtain ⟨X₁, ⟨e₁⟩⟩ : ∃ X₁, Nonempty ((L').obj X₁ ≅ Y₁) := ⟨_, ⟨(L').objObjPreimageIso Y₁⟩⟩
332	  obtain ⟨X₂, ⟨e₂⟩⟩ : ∃ X₂, Nonempty ((L').obj X₂ ≅ Y₂) := ⟨_, ⟨(L').objObjPreimageIso Y₂⟩⟩
333	  obtain ⟨X₃, ⟨e₃⟩⟩ : ∃ X₃, Nonempty ((L').obj X₃ ≅ Y₃) := ⟨_, ⟨(L').objObjPreimageIso Y₃⟩⟩
334	  obtain ⟨X₄, ⟨e₄⟩⟩ : ∃ X₄, Nonempty ((L').obj X₄ ≅ Y₄) := ⟨_, ⟨(L').objObjPreimageIso Y₄⟩⟩
335	  suffices Pentagon ((L').obj X₁) ((L').obj X₂) ((L').obj X₃) ((L').obj X₄) by
336	    dsimp [Pentagon]
337	    refine Eq.trans ?_ (((((e₁.inv ⊗ₘ e₂.inv) ⊗ₘ e₃.inv) ⊗ₘ e₄.inv) ≫= this =≫
338	      (e₁.hom ⊗ₘ e₂.hom ⊗ₘ e₃.hom ⊗ₘ e₄.hom)).trans ?_)
339	    · rw [← id_tensorHom, ← id_tensorHom, ← tensorHom_id, ← tensorHom_id, assoc, assoc,
340	        ← tensor_comp, ← associator_naturality, id_comp, ← comp_id e₁.hom,
341	        tensor_comp, ← associator_naturality_assoc, ← comp_id (𝟙 ((L').obj X₄)),
342	        ← tensor_comp_assoc, associator_naturality, comp_id, comp_id,
343	        ← tensor_comp_assoc, assoc, e₄.inv_hom_id, ← tensor_comp, e₁.inv_hom_id,
344	        ← tensor_comp, e₂.inv_hom_id, e₃.inv_hom_id, id_tensorHom_id, id_tensorHom_id, comp_id]
345	    · rw [assoc, associator_naturality_assoc, associator_naturality_assoc,
346	        ← tensor_comp, e₁.inv_hom_id, ← tensor_comp, e₂.inv_hom_id, ← tensor_comp,
347	        e₃.inv_hom_id, e₄.inv_hom_id, id_tensorHom_id, id_tensorHom_id, id_tensorHom_id, comp_id]
348	  dsimp [Pentagon]
349	  have : ((L').obj X₁ ◁ (μ L W ε X₂ X₃).inv) ▷ (L').obj X₄ ≫
350	      (α_ ((L').obj X₁) ((L').obj X₂ ⊗ (L').obj X₃) ((L').obj X₄)).hom ≫
351	        (L').obj X₁ ◁ (μ L W ε X₂ X₃).hom ▷ (L').obj X₄ =
352	          (α_ ((L').obj X₁) ((L').obj (X₂ ⊗ X₃)) ((L').obj X₄)).hom :=
353	    pentagon_aux₂ _ _ _ (μ L W ε X₂ X₃).symm
354	  rw [associator_hom_app, tensorHom_id, id_tensorHom, associator_hom_app, tensorHom_id,
355	    whiskerLeft_comp, whiskerRight_comp, whiskerRight_comp, whiskerRight_comp, assoc, assoc,
356	    assoc, whiskerRight_comp, assoc,
357	    reassoc_of% this, associator_hom_app, tensorHom_id,
358	    ← pentagon_aux₁ (X₂ := (L').obj X₃) (X₃ := (L').obj X₄) (i := μ L W ε X₁ X₂),
359	    ← pentagon_aux₃ (X₁ := (L').obj X₁) (X₂ := (L').obj X₂) (i := μ L W ε X₃ X₄),
360	    associator_hom_app, associator_hom_app]
361	  simp only [assoc, ← whiskerRight_comp_assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, comp_id, μ_natural_left_assoc,
362	    id_tensorHom, ← whiskerLeft_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
363	  rw [← (L').map_comp_assoc, whiskerLeft_comp, μ_inv_natural_right_assoc, ← (L').map_comp_assoc]
364	  simp only [assoc, MonoidalCategory.pentagon, Functor.map_comp, tensorHom_id,
365	    whiskerRight_comp_assoc]
366	  congr 3; simp only [← assoc]; congr
367	  simp only [← cancel_mono (μ L W ε (X₁ ⊗ X₂) (X₃ ⊗ X₄)).inv, assoc, id_comp,
368	    whisker_exchange_assoc, ← whiskerRight_comp_assoc,
369	    Iso.inv_hom_id, whiskerRight_id, ← whiskerLeft_comp,
370	    whiskerLeft_id]

382	lemma triangle_aux₁ {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε}
383	    (i₁ : X₁ ≅ Y₁) (i₂ : X₂ ≅ Y₂) (i₃ : X₃ ≅ Y₃) :
384	    ((i₁.hom ⊗ₘ i₂.hom) ⊗ₘ i₃.hom) ≫ (α_ Y₁ Y₂ Y₃).hom ≫ (i₁.inv ⊗ₘ i₂.inv ⊗ₘ i₃.inv) =
385	      (α_ X₁ X₂ X₃).hom := by

387	    whiskerLeft_id, comp_id]

389	lemma triangle_aux₂ {X Y : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} {X' Y' : C}
390	    (e₁ : (L').obj X' ≅ X) (e₂ : (L').obj Y' ≅ Y) :
391	      e₁.hom ⊗ₘ (ε.hom ⊗ₘ e₂.hom) ≫ (λ_ Y).hom =
392	        (L').obj X' ◁ ((ε' L W ε).hom ▷ (L').obj Y' ≫
393	          𝟙_ _ ◁ e₂.hom ≫ (λ_ Y).hom) ≫ e₁.hom ▷ Y := by
394	  simp only [← tensorHom_id, ← id_tensorHom, ← tensor_comp, comp_id, id_comp,

396	  congr 3
397	  exact (comp_id _).symm

399	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
400	lemma triangle_aux₃ {X Y : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε} {X' Y' : C}
401	    (e₁ : (L').obj X' ≅ X) (e₂ : (L').obj Y' ≅ Y) : (ρ_ X).hom ▷ _ =
402	      ((e₁.inv ⊗ₘ ε.inv) ⊗ₘ e₂.inv) ≫ _ ◁ e₂.hom ≫ ((μ L W ε X' (𝟙_ C)).hom ≫
403	        (L').map (ρ_ X').hom) ▷ Y ≫ e₁.hom ▷ Y := by
404	  simp only [← tensorHom_id, ← id_tensorHom, ← tensor_comp, assoc, comp_id,
405	    id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id]
406	  congr
407	  rw [← cancel_mono e₁.inv, assoc, assoc, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id,
408	    ← rightUnitor_naturality, rightUnitor_hom_app,
409	    ← tensorHom_id, ← id_tensorHom, ← tensor_comp_assoc, comp_id, id_comp]
