411	variable {L W ε} in
412	lemma triangle (X Y : LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :
413	    (α_ X (𝟙_ _) Y).hom ≫ X ◁ (λ_ Y).hom = (ρ_ X).hom ▷ Y := by
414	  obtain ⟨X', ⟨e₁⟩⟩ : ∃ X₁, Nonempty ((L').obj X₁ ≅ X) := ⟨_, ⟨(L').objObjPreimageIso X⟩⟩
415	  obtain ⟨Y', ⟨e₂⟩⟩ : ∃ X₂, Nonempty ((L').obj X₂ ≅ Y) := ⟨_, ⟨(L').objObjPreimageIso Y⟩⟩
416	  have h₁ := (associator_hom_app L W ε X' (𝟙_ _) Y' =≫
417	    (𝟙 ((L').obj X') ⊗ₘ (μ L W ε (𝟙_ C) Y').hom))
418	  simp only [assoc, id_tensorHom, ← whiskerLeft_comp,
419	    Iso.inv_hom_id, whiskerLeft_id, comp_id, Iso.inv_hom_id,
420	    ← cancel_mono (μ L W ε X' (𝟙_ C ⊗ Y')).hom] at h₁
421	  have h₂ := (ε' L W ε).hom ▷ (L').obj Y' ≫= leftUnitor_hom_app L W ε Y'
422	  simp only [← whiskerRight_comp_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, whiskerRight_id, id_comp] at h₂
423	  have h₃ := (((μ L W ε _ _).hom ⊗ₘ 𝟙 _) ≫ (μ L W ε _ _).hom) ≫=
424	    ((L').congr_map (MonoidalCategory.triangle X' Y'))
425	  simp only [assoc, Functor.map_comp, ← reassoc_of% h₁] at h₃
426	  rw [← μ_natural_left, tensorHom_id, ← whiskerRight_comp_assoc,
427	    ← μ_natural_right, ← Iso.comp_inv_eq, assoc, assoc, assoc,
428	    Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id, ← whiskerLeft_comp, ← h₂] at h₃
429	  replace h₃ := ((e₁.inv ⊗ₘ ε.inv) ⊗ₘ e₂.inv) ≫= (h₃ =≫ (_ ◁ e₂.hom)) =≫ (e₁.hom ▷ _)
430	  simp only [← whiskerLeft_comp, assoc, ← leftUnitor_naturality, ← whisker_exchange] at h₃
431	  have : _ = (α_ X (𝟙_ (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε)) Y).hom :=
432	    triangle_aux₁ _ _ _ e₁.symm ε.symm e₂.symm
433	  simp only [← this, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.symm_inv, assoc,
434	    ← id_tensorHom, ← tensor_comp, comp_id]
435	  convert h₃
436	  · exact triangle_aux₂ _ _ _ e₁ e₂
437	  · exact triangle_aux₃ _ _ _ e₁ e₂

441	    MonoidalCategory (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) where
442	  tensorHom_def := by intros; simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
443	  id_tensorHom_id := by
444	    intros
445	    simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
446	  tensorHom_comp_tensorHom := by intros; simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
447	  whiskerLeft_id := by
448	    intros
449	    simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
450	  id_whiskerRight := by
451	    intros
452	    simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
453	  associator_naturality {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₁ Y₂ Y₃} f₁ f₂ f₃ := by apply associator_naturality
454	  leftUnitor_naturality := by intros; simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
455	  rightUnitor_naturality := fun f ↦ (rightUnitor L W ε).hom.naturality f
456	  pentagon := pentagon
457	  triangle := triangle

465	noncomputable instance : (toMonoidalCategory L W ε).Monoidal :=
466	  Functor.CoreMonoidal.toMonoidal
467	    { εIso := ε.symm
468	      μIso X Y := μ L W ε X Y
469	      associativity X Y Z := by simp [associator_hom_app L W ε X Y Z]
470	      left_unitality Y := leftUnitor_hom_app L W ε Y
471	      right_unitality X := rightUnitor_hom_app L W ε X }
472	
473	local notation "L'" => toMonoidalCategory L W ε
474	
475	lemma associator_hom (X Y Z : C) :
476	    (α_ ((L').obj X) ((L').obj Y) ((L').obj Z)).hom =
477	    (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') X Y) ▷ (L').obj Z ≫
478	      (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') (X ⊗ Y) Z) ≫
479	        (L').map (α_ X Y Z).hom ≫
480	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') X (Y ⊗ Z)) ≫
481	            ((L').obj X) ◁ (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') Y Z) := by
482	  simp
483	
484	lemma associator_inv (X Y Z : C) :
485	    (α_ ((L').obj X) ((L').obj Y) ((L').obj Z)).inv =
486	    (L').obj X ◁ (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') Y Z) ≫
487	      (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') X (Y ⊗ Z)) ≫
488	        (L').map (α_ X Y Z).inv ≫
489	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') (X ⊗ Y) Z) ≫
490	            (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') X Y) ▷ ((L').obj Z) := by
491	  simp
492	
493	
494	end CategoryTheory

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.CatCommSq
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Opposite
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.FullyFaithful
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Opposites
12	
13	/-!
14	# Localization of adjunctions
15	
16	In this file, we show that if we have an adjunction `adj : G ⊣ F` such that both
17	functors `G : C₁ ⥤ C₂` and `F : C₂ ⥤ C₁` induce functors
18	`G' : D₁ ⥤ D₂` and `F' : D₂ ⥤ D₁` on localized categories, i.e. that we
19	have localization functors `L₁ : C₁ ⥤ D₁` and `L₂ : C₂ ⥤ D₂` with respect
20	to morphism properties `W₁` and `W₂` respectively, and 2-commutative diagrams
21	`[CatCommSq G L₁ L₂ G']` and `[CatCommSq F L₂ L₁ F']`, then we have an
22	induced adjunction `Adjunction.localization L₁ W₁ L₂ W₂ G' F' : G' ⊣ F'`.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	open Localization Category Functor
31	
32	namespace Adjunction
33	
34	variable {C₁ C₂ D₁ D₂ : Type*} [Category* C₁] [Category* C₂] [Category* D₁] [Category* D₂]
35	  {G : C₁ ⥤ C₂} {F : C₂ ⥤ C₁} (adj : G ⊣ F)
36	
37	section
38	
39	variable (L₁ : C₁ ⥤ D₁) (W₁ : MorphismProperty C₁) [L₁.IsLocalization W₁]
40	  (L₂ : C₂ ⥤ D₂) (W₂ : MorphismProperty C₂) [L₂.IsLocalization W₂]
41	  (G' : D₁ ⥤ D₂) (F' : D₂ ⥤ D₁)
42	  [CatCommSq G L₁ L₂ G'] [CatCommSq F L₂ L₁ F']
43	
44	
45	namespace Localization
46	
47	/-- Auxiliary definition of the unit morphism for the adjunction `Adjunction.localization` -/
48	noncomputable def ε : 𝟭 D₁ ⟶ G' ⋙ F' := by
49	  letI : Lifting L₁ W₁ ((G ⋙ F) ⋙ L₁) (G' ⋙ F') :=
50	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp G F L₁ L₂ L₁ G' F').iso.symm
51	  exact Localization.liftNatTrans L₁ W₁ L₁ ((G ⋙ F) ⋙ L₁) (𝟭 D₁) (G' ⋙ F')
52	    (whiskerRight adj.unit L₁)
53	
54	lemma ε_app (X₁ : C₁) :
55	    (ε adj L₁ W₁ L₂ G' F').app (L₁.obj X₁) =
56	      L₁.map (adj.unit.app X₁) ≫ (CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').hom.app (G.obj X₁) ≫
57	        F'.map ((CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').hom.app X₁) := by
58	  letI : Lifting L₁ W₁ ((G ⋙ F) ⋙ L₁) (G' ⋙ F') :=
59	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp G F L₁ L₂ L₁ G' F').iso.symm
60	  simp only [ε, liftNatTrans_app, Lifting.iso, Iso.symm,
61	    Functor.id_obj, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app,
62	      whiskerRight_app, CatCommSq.hComp_iso_hom_app, id_comp]
63	
64	/-- Auxiliary definition of the counit morphism for the adjunction `Adjunction.localization` -/
65	noncomputable def η : F' ⋙ G' ⟶ 𝟭 D₂ := by
66	  letI : Lifting L₂ W₂ ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ L₂) (F' ⋙ G') :=
67	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp F G L₂ L₁ L₂ F' G').iso.symm
68	  exact liftNatTrans L₂ W₂ ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ L₂) L₂ (F' ⋙ G') (𝟭 D₂) (whiskerRight adj.counit L₂)
69	
70	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
71	lemma η_app (X₂ : C₂) :
72	    (η adj L₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').app (L₂.obj X₂) =
73	      G'.map ((CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').inv.app X₂) ≫
74	        (CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').inv.app (F.obj X₂) ≫
75	        L₂.map (adj.counit.app X₂) := by
76	  letI : Lifting L₂ W₂ ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ L₂) (F' ⋙ G') :=
77	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp F G L₂ L₁ L₂ F' G').iso.symm
78	  simp only [η, liftNatTrans_app, Lifting.iso, Iso.symm, CatCommSq.hComp_iso_inv_app,
79	    whiskerRight_app, Functor.rightUnitor_inv_app, comp_id, assoc]
80	
81	end Localization
82	
83	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
84	/-- If `adj : G ⊣ F` is an adjunction between two categories `C₁` and `C₂` that
85	are equipped with localization functors `L₁ : C₁ ⥤ D₁` and `L₂ : C₂ ⥤ D₂` with
86	respect to `W₁ : MorphismProperty C₁` and `W₂ : MorphismProperty C₂`, and that
87	the functors `F : C₂ ⥤ C₁` and `G : C₁ ⥤ C₂` induce functors `F' : D₂ ⥤ D₁`
88	and `G' : D₁ ⥤ D₂` on the localized categories, then the adjunction `adj`
89	induces an adjunction `G' ⊣ F'`. -/
90	noncomputable def localization : G' ⊣ F' :=
91	  Adjunction.mkOfUnitCounit
92	    { unit := Localization.ε adj L₁ W₁ L₂ G' F'
93	      counit := Localization.η adj L₁ L₂ W₂ G' F'
94	      left_triangle := by
95	        apply natTrans_ext L₁ W₁
96	        intro X₁
97	        have eq := congr_app adj.left_triangle X₁
98	        dsimp at eq
99	        rw [NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.comp_app, whiskerRight_app, Localization.ε_app,
100	          Functor.associator_hom_app, id_comp, whiskerLeft_app, G'.map_comp, G'.map_comp,
101	          assoc, assoc]
102	        erw [(Localization.η adj L₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').naturality, Localization.η_app,
103	          assoc, assoc, ← G'.map_comp_assoc, ← G'.map_comp_assoc, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app,
104	          comp_id, (CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').inv.naturality_assoc, ← L₂.map_comp_assoc, eq,
105	          L₂.map_id, id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
106	        rfl
107	      right_triangle := by
108	        apply natTrans_ext L₂ W₂
109	        intro X₂
110	        have eq := congr_app adj.right_triangle X₂
111	        dsimp at eq
112	        rw [NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.comp_app, whiskerLeft_app, whiskerRight_app,
113	          Localization.η_app, Functor.associator_inv_app, id_comp, F'.map_comp, F'.map_comp]
114	        erw [← (Localization.ε _ _ _ _ _ _).naturality_assoc, Localization.ε_app,
115	          assoc, assoc, ← F'.map_comp_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app, F'.map_id, id_comp,
116	          ← NatTrans.naturality, ← L₁.map_comp_assoc, eq, L₁.map_id, id_comp,
117	          Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
118	        rfl }
119	
120	@[simp]
121	lemma localization_unit_app (X₁ : C₁) :
122	    (adj.localization L₁ W₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').unit.app (L₁.obj X₁) =
123	    L₁.map (adj.unit.app X₁) ≫ (CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').hom.app (G.obj X₁) ≫
124	      F'.map ((CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').hom.app X₁) := by
125	  apply Localization.ε_app
126	
127	@[simp]
128	lemma localization_counit_app (X₂ : C₂) :
129	    (adj.localization L₁ W₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').counit.app (L₂.obj X₂) =
130	    G'.map ((CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').inv.app X₂) ≫
131	      (CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').inv.app (F.obj X₂) ≫
132	      L₂.map (adj.counit.app X₂) := by
133	  apply Localization.η_app
134	
135	end
136	
137	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
138	include adj in
139	lemma isLocalization [F.Full] [F.Faithful] :
140	    G.IsLocalization ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C₂).inverseImage G) := by
141	  let W := ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C₂).inverseImage G)
142	  have hG : W.IsInvertedBy G := fun _ _ _ hf => hf
143	  have : ∀ (X : C₁), IsIso ((whiskerRight adj.unit W.Q).app X) := fun X =>
144	    Localization.inverts W.Q W _ (by
145	      change IsIso _
146	      infer_instance)
147	  have : IsIso (whiskerRight adj.unit W.Q) := NatIso.isIso_of_isIso_app _
148	  let e : W.Localization ≌ C₂ := Equivalence.mk (Localization.lift G hG W.Q) (F ⋙ W.Q)
149	    (liftNatIso W.Q W W.Q (G ⋙ F ⋙ W.Q) _ _
150	    (W.Q.leftUnitor.symm ≪≫ asIso (whiskerRight adj.unit W.Q)))
151	    (Functor.associator _ _ _ ≪≫ isoWhiskerLeft _ (Localization.fac G hG W.Q) ≪≫
152	      asIso adj.counit)
153	  apply Functor.IsLocalization.of_equivalence_target W.Q W G e
154	    (Localization.fac G hG W.Q)
155	
156	include adj in
157	/-- This is the dual statement to `Adjunction.isLocalization`. -/
158	lemma isLocalization' [G.Full] [G.Faithful] :
159	    F.IsLocalization ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C₁).inverseImage F) := by
160	  rw [← Functor.IsLocalization.op_iff, MorphismProperty.op_inverseImage,
161	    MorphismProperty.op_isomorphisms]
162	  exact adj.op.isLocalization
163	
164	end Adjunction
165	
166	end CategoryTheory
167

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Abelian
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafify
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Limits
11	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Limits
12	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallyBijective
13	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheafification
14	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced
15	
16	/-!
17	# The sheafification functor for presheaves of modules
18	
19	In this file, we construct a functor
20	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification α : PresheafOfModules R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules R`
21	for a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
22	and `R` a sheaf of rings.
23	In particular, if `α` is the identity of `R.val`, we obtain the
24	sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules R.val ⥤ SheafOfModules R`.
25	
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	universe v v' u u'
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Category Limits
33	
34	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
35	  {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R.obj)
36	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
37	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
38	
39	namespace PresheafOfModules
40	
41	instance : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
42	  have : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R ⋙ sheafToPresheaf _ _).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
43	    inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ toPresheaf _).ReflectsIsomorphisms
44	  reflectsIsomorphisms_of_comp _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)
45	
46	section
47	
48	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
49	
50	/-- Given a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
51	and `R` a sheaf of rings (i.e. `R` identifies to the sheafification of `R₀`), this is
52	the associated sheaf of modules functor `PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R`. -/
53	@[simps! -isSimp map]
54	noncomputable def sheafification : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R where
55	  obj M₀ := sheafify α (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J M₀.presheaf)
56	  map f := sheafifyMap _ _ _ f
57	    ((toPresheaf R₀ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat).map f)
58	      (by apply toSheafify_naturality)
59	  map_id M₀ := by
60	    ext1
61	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
62	    simp
63	    rfl
64	  map_comp _ _ := by
65	    ext1
66	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
67	    simp
68	    rfl
69	
70	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
71	forgets the module structures. -/
72	noncomputable def sheafificationCompToSheaf :
73	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf _ ≅
74	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
75	  Iso.refl _
76	
77	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
78	forgets the module structures. -/
79	noncomputable def sheafificationCompForgetCompToPresheaf :
80	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅
81	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
82	  Iso.refl _
83	
84	/-- The bijection between types of morphisms which is part of the adjunction
85	`sheafificationAdjunction`. -/
86	noncomputable def sheafificationHomEquiv
87	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R} :
88	    ((sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) ≃
89	      (P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) := by
90	  apply sheafifyHomEquiv
91	
92	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def
93	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
94	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
95	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
96	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P.presheaf ≫ (toPresheaf R.obj).map f.val := rfl
97	
98	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
99	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv
100	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
101	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
102	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
103	      (sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv P.presheaf
104	        ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).obj F) ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map f) := by
105	  rw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
106	  dsimp
107	
108	lemma toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm
109	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
110	    (g : P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) :
111	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map ((sheafificationHomEquiv α).symm g) =
112	      (((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv
113	        P.presheaf ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).obj F)).symm ((toPresheaf R₀).map g)) := by
114	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := (sheafificationHomEquiv α).surjective g
115	  apply ((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv _ _).injective
116	  rw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
117	  rfl
118	
119	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
120	/-- Given a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
121	and `R` a sheaf of rings, this is the adjunction
122	`sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α`. -/
123	noncomputable def sheafificationAdjunction :
124	    sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α :=
125	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
126	    { homEquiv := fun _ _ ↦ sheafificationHomEquiv α
127	      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm := fun {P₀ Q₀ N} f g ↦ by
128	        apply (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map_injective
129	        rw [Functor.map_comp]
130	        erw [toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm,
131	          toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm α g]
132	        rw [Functor.map_comp]
133	        apply (CategoryTheory.sheafificationAdjunction J
134	          AddCommGrpCat.{v}).homEquiv_naturality_left_symm
135	      homEquiv_naturality_right := fun {P₀ M N} f g ↦ by
136	        apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
137	        erw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv] }
138	
139	lemma sheafificationAdjunction_homEquiv_apply {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀}
140	    {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
141	    (sheafificationAdjunction α).homEquiv P F f = sheafificationHomEquiv α f := rfl
142	
143	@[simp]
144	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app (M₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀) :
145	    (toPresheaf _).map ((sheafificationAdjunction α).unit.app M₀) =
146	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify J M₀.presheaf := rfl
147	
148	@[simp]
149	lemma toSheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_counit_app (M : SheafOfModules.{v} R) :
150	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).map ((sheafificationAdjunction α).counit.app M) =
151	      (CategoryTheory.sheafificationAdjunction J
152	          AddCommGrpCat.{v}).counit.app ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).obj M) :=
153	  (toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm _ _).trans
154	    (by rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_id]; rfl)
155	
156	instance : (sheafification.{v} α).IsLeftAdjoint :=
157	  (sheafificationAdjunction α).isLeftAdjoint
158	
159	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
160	instance : IsIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit := by
161	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
162	  intro F
163	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R)]
164	  simp only [Functor.comp_obj, SheafOfModules.forget_obj, restrictScalars_obj, Functor.id_obj,
165	    toSheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_counit_app]
166	  infer_instance
167	
168	instance : (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ restrictScalars α).Full :=
169	  (sheafificationAdjunction.{v} α).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
170	
171	instance : (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ restrictScalars α).Faithful :=
172	  (sheafificationAdjunction.{v} α).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
173	
174	end
175	
176	section
177	
178	variable [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
179	
180	noncomputable instance :
181	    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R) :=
182	  comp_preservesFiniteLimits (toPresheaf.{v} R₀) (presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat)
183	
184	instance : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
185	  reflectsIsomorphisms_of_comp (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R) (sheafToPresheaf J _)
186	
187	noncomputable instance : ReflectsFiniteLimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R) where
188	  reflects _ _ _ := inferInstance
189	
190	noncomputable instance : PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafification.{v} α) :=
191	  preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves
192	    (sheafification.{v} α) (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R)
193	
194	end
195	
196	end PresheafOfModules

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
32	
33	variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52	
53	section Functor
54	
55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
79	
80	end Functor

84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule

94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119

121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130

133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]

150	/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
151	def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
152	  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom
153	
154	@[simp]
155	lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
156	    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
157	
158	@[simp]
159	lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
160	    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl
161	
162	@[simp]
163	lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
164	    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
165	
166	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
167	def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
168	  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom
169	
170	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
171	is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
172	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
173	  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _
174	
175	section
176	
177	attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
178	  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts
179	
180	instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
181	instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
182	instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
183	instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

188	/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
189	to the identity functor. -/
190	def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
191	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
192	
193	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

197	/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
198	to the identity functor. -/

203	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
204	    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
205	      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
206	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _
207	
208	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
209	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
210	def pushforwardComp :
211	    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
212	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _
213	
214	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
215	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
216	
217	/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
218	identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
219	def pullbackComp :
220	    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
221	  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _
222	
223	/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
224	def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
225	    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
226	      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
227	
228	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
229	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
230	
231	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
232	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
233	
234	/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
235	def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
236	  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)
237	
238	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
239	    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
240	      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
241	    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
242	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _
243	
244	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
245	@[reassoc]
246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
247	    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
248	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
250	    eqToHom (by simp) := by
251	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
252	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
258	      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
259	  simp [this]
260	
261	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
262	@[reassoc]
263	lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
264	    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
265	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
266	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
267	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
268	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
269	  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
270	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
271	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
272	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
273	  simp [← this]
274	
275	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
276	@[reassoc]
277	lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
278	    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
279	      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
280	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
281	  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
282	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
283	  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
284	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
285	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
286	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
287	  simp [← this]

290	attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
291	  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
292	  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
293	/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
294	a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
295	(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
296	these categories.) -/
297	@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
298	  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
299	  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
300	def pseudofunctor :
301	    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
302	  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
303	    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
304	    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
305	    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
306	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
307	    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
308	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))
309	
310	end Functorial

314	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]
315	
316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
321	  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩
323	
324	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
325	abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
326	  (restrictFunctor f).obj M
327	
328	@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
329	    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl
330	
331	@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333	
334	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)
352	
353	instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
354	  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)
355	
356	instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
357	instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
358	instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
359	
360	@[simp]
361	lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
362	    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
363	      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl
364	
365	@[simp]
366	lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
367	    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
368	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl
369	
370	/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
371	def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
372	  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
373	
374	/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
375	def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
376	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
377	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
378	      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
379	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
380	
381	@[simp]
382	lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
383	    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
384	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl
385	
386	@[simp]
387	lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
388	    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
389	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl
390	
391	/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
392	def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
393	  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
394	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
395	    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
396	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
397	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
398	    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm
399	
400	@[simp]
401	lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
402	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
403	
404	@[simp]
405	lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
406	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
407	
408	/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
409	def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
410	    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
411	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
412	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)
413	
414	@[simp]
415	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
416	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
417	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
418	
419	@[simp]
420	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
421	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
422	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
423	
424	/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
425	def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
426	    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
427	    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
428	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
429	  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
430	      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
431	      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)
432	
433	@[simp]
434	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
435	    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
436	      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
437	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
438	  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
439	    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
440	    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _
441	
442	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
443	@[simp]
444	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
445	    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
446	      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
447	  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
448	    Iso.hom_inv_id]
449	  simp
450	
451	end Restriction
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

6	module

8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Closed.FunctorCategory.Basic

10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Equivalence

13	/-!
14	# Monoidal category structure on categories of sheaves
15	
16	If `A` is a closed braided category with suitable limits,
17	and `J` is a Grothendieck topology with `HasWeakSheafify J A`,
18	then `Sheaf J A` can be equipped with a monoidal category
19	structure. This is not made an instance as in some cases
20	it may conflict with monoidal structure deduced from
21	chosen finite products.
22	
23	## TODO
24	
25	* show that the monoidal category structure on sheaves is closed,
26	  and that the internal hom can be defined in such a way that the
27	  underlying presheaf is the internal hom in the category of presheaves.
28	  Note that a `MonoidalClosed` instance on sheaves can already be obtained
29	  abstractly using the material in `CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Braided.Reflection`.
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	universe v₁ v₂ v₃ u₁ u₂ u₃
36	
37	namespace CategoryTheory
38	
39	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
40	  {A : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} A] [MonoidalCategory A]
41	
42	open Opposite Limits MonoidalCategory MonoidalClosed Enriched.FunctorCategory
43	
44	namespace Presheaf
45	
46	variable [MonoidalClosed A]
47	
48	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
49	/-- Relation between `functorEnrichedHom` and `presheafHom`. -/
50	noncomputable def functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv (M : A) (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)
51	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G] (X : C) :
52	    (functorEnrichedHom A F G ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op M)).obj (op X) ≃
53	    (presheafHom (F ⊗ (Functor.const _).obj M) G).obj (op X) where
54	  toFun f :=
55	    { app j := MonoidalClosed.uncurry (f ≫ enrichedHomπ A _ _ (Under.mk j.unop.hom.op))
56	      naturality j j' φ := by
57	        dsimp
58	        rw [tensorHom_id, ← uncurry_natural_right, ← uncurry_pre_app, Category.assoc,
59	          Category.assoc, ← enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerRight,
60	          ← enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerLeft]
61	        congr 2
62	        exact (enrichedHom_condition A (Under.forget (op X) ⋙ F) (Under.forget (op X) ⋙ G)
63	          (i := Under.mk j.unop.hom.op) (j := Under.mk j'.unop.hom.op)
64	            (Under.homMk φ.unop.left.op (Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (by simp)))).symm }
65	  invFun g :=
66	    end_.lift (fun j ↦ MonoidalClosed.curry (g.app (op (Over.mk j.hom.unop)))) (fun j j' φ ↦ by
67	      dsimp
68	      rw [enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerRight,
69	        enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerLeft,
70	        curry_pre_app, ← curry_natural_right]
71	      congr 1
72	      let α : Over.mk j'.hom.unop ⟶ Over.mk j.hom.unop := Over.homMk φ.right.unop
73	        (Quiver.Hom.op_inj (by simp))
74	      simpa using (g.naturality α.op).symm)
75	  left_inv f := by
76	    dsimp
77	    ext j
78	    dsimp
79	    simp only [curry_uncurry, end_.lift_π]
80	    rfl
81	  right_inv g := by
82	    dsimp
83	    ext j
84	    dsimp
85	    simp only [uncurry_curry, end_.lift_π]
86	    rfl
87	
88	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
89	lemma functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv_naturality
90	    {M : A} {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y)
91	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G]
92	    (y : (functorEnrichedHom A F G ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op M)).obj (op Y)) :
93	    functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G X
94	      (y ≫ precompEnrichedHom' _ (Under.map f.op) (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)) =
95	    (presheafHom (F ⊗ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj M) G).map f.op
96	      (functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G Y y) := by
97	  dsimp
98	  ext ⟨j⟩
99	  simp [functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv, presheafHom]
100	  rfl
101	
102	lemma isSheaf_functorEnrichedHom (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) (hG : Presheaf.IsSheaf J G)
103	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G] :
104	    Presheaf.IsSheaf J (functorEnrichedHom A F G) := fun M ↦ by
105	  rw [Presieve.isSheaf_iff_of_nat_equiv
106	    (functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G)
107	    (fun _ _ _ _ ↦ functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv_naturality _ _)]
108	  rw [← isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
109	  exact Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom (F ⊗ (Functor.const _).obj M) G hG
110	
111	end Presheaf

113	namespace GrothendieckTopology
114	
115	namespace W
116	
117	variable (J A) in
118	lemma transport_isMonoidal {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] (K : GrothendieckTopology D)
119	    (G : D ⥤ C) [G.IsCoverDense J] [G.Full] [G.IsContinuous K J]
120	    [(G.sheafPushforwardContinuous A K J).EssSurj] [(K.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] :
121	    (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal := by
122	  rw [← J.W_inverseImage_whiskeringLeft K G]
123	  infer_instance
124	
125	variable [MonoidalClosed A]
126	  [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
127	  [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
128	
129	open MonoidalClosed.FunctorCategory
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma whiskerLeft {G₁ G₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {g : G₁ ⟶ G₂} (hg : J.W g) (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :
133	    J.W (F ◁ g) := fun H h ↦ by
134	  have := hg _ (Presheaf.isSheaf_functorEnrichedHom F H h)
135	  rw [← Function.Bijective.of_comp_iff' (f := MonoidalClosed.curry)
136	    ((ihom.adjunction _).homEquiv _ _).bijective]
137	  rw [← Function.Bijective.of_comp_iff (g := MonoidalClosed.curry) _
138	    ((ihom.adjunction _).homEquiv _ _).bijective] at this
139	  convert this using 1
140	  ext α : 1
141	  dsimp
142	  rw [curry_natural_left]
143	
144	lemma whiskerRight [BraidedCategory A]
145	    {F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {f : F₁ ⟶ F₂} (hf : J.W f) (G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :
146	    J.W (f ▷ G) :=
147	  (J.W.arrow_mk_iso_iff (Arrow.isoMk (β_ F₁ G) (β_ F₂ G))).2 (hf.whiskerLeft G)
148	
149	instance monoidal [BraidedCategory A] : (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal where
150	  whiskerLeft F _ _ _ hg := hg.whiskerLeft F
151	  whiskerRight _ hf G := hf.whiskerRight G
152	
153	end W
154	
155	end GrothendieckTopology
156	
157	namespace Sheaf
158	
159	variable (J A)
160	
161	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` that is obtained
162	by localization of the monoidal category structure on the category
163	of presheaves. -/
164	@[instance_reducible]
165	noncomputable def monoidalCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
166	    MonoidalCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
167	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory
168	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
169	
170	attribute [local instance] monoidalCategory
171	
172	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` obtained in `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` is
173	braided when `A` is braided. -/
174	@[implicit_reducible]
175	noncomputable def braidedCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
176	    [BraidedCategory A] : BraidedCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
177	  inferInstanceAs (BraidedCategory
178	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
179	
180	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` obtained in `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` is
181	symmetric when `A` is symmetric. -/
182	@[implicit_reducible]
183	noncomputable def symmetricCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
184	    [SymmetricCategory A] :
185	    SymmetricCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
186	  inferInstanceAs (SymmetricCategory
187	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
188	
189	noncomputable instance [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
190	    (presheafToSheaf J A).Monoidal :=
191	  inferInstanceAs (Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory
192	    (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)).Monoidal
193	
194	noncomputable instance [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] [BraidedCategory A] :
195	    letI := braidedCategory J A
196	    (presheafToSheaf J A).Braided :=
197	  inferInstanceAs (Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory
198	    (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)).Braided
199	
200	noncomputable example
201	    [HasWeakSheafify J A] [MonoidalClosed A] [BraidedCategory A]
202	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
203	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂] :
204	    MonoidalCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
205	  monoidalCategory J A
206	
207	noncomputable example
208	    [HasWeakSheafify J A] [MonoidalClosed A] [BraidedCategory A]
209	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
210	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂] :
211	    BraidedCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
212	  braidedCategory J A
213	
214	noncomputable example
215	    [HasWeakSheafify J A] [MonoidalClosed A] [SymmetricCategory A]
216	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
217	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂] :
218	    SymmetricCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
219	  symmetricCategory J A

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Colimits
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed
10	
11	/-!
12	# The monoidal category structure on presheaves of modules
13	
14	Given a presheaf of commutative rings `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat`, we construct
15	the monoidal category structure on the category of presheaves of modules
16	`PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`. The tensor product `M₁ ⊗ M₂` is defined
17	as the presheaf of modules which sends `X : Cᵒᵖ` to `M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X`.
18	
19	## Notes
20	
21	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop
22	"Formalizing algebraic geometry" in June 2024.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory BraidedCategory Category Limits
29	
30	universe v u v₁ u₁
31	
32	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
33	
34	instance (X : Cᵒᵖ) : CommRing ((R ⋙ forget₂ _ RingCat).obj X) :=
35	  inferInstanceAs (CommRing (R.obj X))
36	
37	namespace PresheafOfModules
38	
39	namespace Monoidal
40	
41	variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
42	
43	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
44	/-- Auxiliary definition for `tensorObj`. -/
45	noncomputable def tensorObjMap {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X ⟶
46	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y) :=
47	  ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorLift (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ M₂.map f m₂)
48	    (by
49	      intro m₁ m₁' m₂
50	      dsimp +instances
51	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul])
52	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₁.map_smul]; rfl)
53	    (by
54	      intro m₁ m₂ m₂'
55	      dsimp +instances
56	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.tmul_add])
57	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₂.map_smul, TensorProduct.tmul_smul (r := R.map f a)]; rfl)
58	
59	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
60	/-- The tensor product of two presheaves of modules. -/
61	@[simps obj]
62	noncomputable def tensorObj : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
63	  obj X := M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X
64	  map f := tensorObjMap M₁ M₂ f
65	  map_id X := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
66	    intro m₁ m₂
67	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
68	    simp
69	    rfl) -- `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App_inv_apply` doesn't get picked up due to type mismatch
70	  map_comp f g := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
71	    intro m₁ m₂
72	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
73	    simp +instances)
74	
75	variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄}
76	
77	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
78	@[simp]
79	lemma tensorObj_map_tmul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (m₁ : M₁.obj X) (m₂ : M₂.obj X) :
80	    DFunLike.coe (α := (M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X :))
81	      (β := fun _ ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y))
82	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((tensorObj M₁ M₂).map f)) (m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj X] m₂) =
83	    M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj Y] M₂.map f m₂ := rfl
84	
85	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
86	/-- The tensor product of two morphisms of presheaves of modules. -/
87	@[simps]
88	noncomputable def tensorHom (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) : tensorObj M₁ M₃ ⟶ tensorObj M₂ M₄ where
89	  app X := f.app X ⊗ₘ g.app X
90	  naturality {X Y} φ := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₃ ↦ by
91	    dsimp
92	    rw [tensorObj_map_tmul]
93	    -- Need `erw` because of the type mismatch in `map` and the tensor product.
94	    erw [ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_tmul, tensorObj_map_tmul]
95	    rw [naturality_apply, naturality_apply]
96	    simp)
97	
98	end Monoidal
99	
100	open Monoidal

34:instance (X : Cᵒᵖ) : CommRing ((R ⋙ forget₂ _ RingCat).obj X) :=
50:      dsimp +instances
55:      dsimp +instances
73:    simp +instances)
104:noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
125:noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
144:instance symmetricCategory :
237:instance (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
243:instance (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :

100	open Monoidal
101	
102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
103	open ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory in
104	noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
105	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
106	  tensorObj := tensorObj
107	  whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
108	  whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
109	  tensorHom := tensorHom
110	  tensorUnit := unit _
111	  associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _)
112	    (fun _ _ _ ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext₃' (by intros; rfl))
113	  leftUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (λ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
114	    ext m
115	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
116	    erw [leftUnitor_inv_apply, leftUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
117	    rfl))
118	  rightUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (ρ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
119	    ext m
120	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
121	    erw [rightUnitor_inv_apply, rightUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
122	    rfl))
123

/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: the tensor
product is `tensorObj`, the tensor unit is the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$,
and the associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited from the presheaf
side through sheafification.

ITER-078 STATUS — STRUCTURED PARTIAL ATTEMPT.

The presheaf-side category `X.PresheafOfModules` carries
`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct`. The data fields `tensorObj`,
`whiskerLeft`, `whiskerRight`, `tensorHom`, and `tensorUnit` can be
constructed by sheafifying the corresponding presheaf-side data through
`L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`, the left
adjoint to the fully-faithful forgetful functor `F := toPresheafOfModules`.

The blocker is the associator/leftUnitor/rightUnitor fields: they require
an iso `tensorObj (tensorObj M N) K ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N K)`. After
unfolding, the LHS is `L (F (L (F M ⊗ F N)) ⊗ F K)` and the RHS is
`L (F M ⊗ F (L (F N ⊗ F K)))`. These differ because `F ∘ L` is the
sheafification monad, not the identity — bridging them requires the
**localization-monoidal machinery** (`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`)
applied to the localization functor `L`, which:

1. Establishes that `L.IsLocalization W` for `W := (isomorphisms _).inverseImage L`
   via `Adjunction.isLocalization` (which is available since the right
   adjoint `F ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)` is Full + Faithful — both already
   instances in Mathlib at `PresheafOfModules/Sheafification.lean:168,171`).
2. Establishes `W.IsMonoidal` — stability of `W` under whiskering. This
   is the substantive new Mathlib content needed: it asserts that if
   `sheafification f` is iso then `sheafification (X ◁ f)` is also iso.
   This holds because sheafification preserves colimits (it is a left
   adjoint) and the presheaf tensor product is a colimit-preserving
   bifunctor on each variable.
3. Then `LocalizedMonoidal L W (Iso.refl _)` provides the full monoidal
   structure on `X.Modules`, matching our definition of `tensorObj`.

The infrastructure pieces (1) and (3) are off-the-shelf; only (2) — the
monoidality of the sheafification's inverted-iso class for
`PresheafOfModules` — is genuinely missing from Mathlib. This single
gap-fill, once supplied, makes the structure and the instance below close
mechanically.

We leave the two `sorry`s in place so the file compiles (sorry-count
budget: 3 → 2; one closure achieved on `tensorObj`). -/

    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) := sorry

/-- The presheaf-to-sheaf sheafification functor for `X.Modules`. Abbreviated `L`
in comments. -/
noncomputable abbrev sheafificationFunctor :
    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⥤ X.Modules :=
  _root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)

/-- The morphism property in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj` consisting of
morphisms inverted by sheafification. By `Adjunction.isLocalization` together with
fullness/faithfulness of the right adjoint (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification`),
the sheafification functor is a localization at this class. -/
noncomputable abbrev W : MorphismProperty
    (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
  (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms _).inverseImage (sheafificationFunctor X)

/-- Sheafification is a localization at `W`. Follows from `Adjunction.isLocalization`
since the right adjoint of `sheafification (𝟙 _)` is Full + Faithful. -/
noncomputable instance sheafificationIsLocalization :
    (sheafificationFunctor X).IsLocalization (W X) :=
  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isLocalization

/-- GAP-FILL: sheafification of `PresheafOfModules` sends presheaf-tensor whiskering
of an iso-inverted morphism to an iso. This is the single semantic gap-fill
needed to invoke the `LocalizedMonoidal` machinery (`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`)
on the sheafification functor.

Mathematical content. The class `W X` of morphisms in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
inverted by sheafification is stable under left- and right-whiskering by any presheaf-of-modules.
This holds because sheafification is a left adjoint to a fully-faithful right adjoint, and the
presheaf tensor product `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight` are colimit-preserving bifunctors on each
variable (see `PresheafOfModules.tensorLeft.PreservesColimitsOfSize` in
`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean:237,243`).

Closing this `IsMonoidal` instance below the level of `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk'` reduces
to the iso-stability of `sheafification` under presheaf-tensor whiskering, which is the analog
for `PresheafOfModules` of `GrothendieckTopology.W.monoidal` for fixed-value sheaves
(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Monoidal.lean:149`). The latter is proved via internal-hom
adjunction; the `PresheafOfModules` version needs the same technique but adapted to the
varying-value-category setting — substantial Mathlib infrastructure not currently present.
Marked sorry until the upstream gap is filled. -/
noncomputable instance instIsMonoidal_W : (W X).IsMonoidal := by
  letI : MonoidalCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
    show MonoidalCategory
      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
      inferInstance
  refine MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk' _ ?_
  intro X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂ f g hf hg
  -- `hf hg` say `sheafification f` and `sheafification g` are isos in `X.Modules`.
  -- Goal: `sheafification (f ⊗ₘ g)` is an iso. Substantive: needs iso-stability of
  -- sheafification under presheaf-tensor whiskering. See declaration docstring.
  sorry

/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: derived from the
`LocalizedMonoidal` machinery applied to the sheafification functor
`L : PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⥤ X.Modules` at the class `W X` of morphisms
inverted by `L`, with unit `L.obj (𝟙_ presheaf)` and `ε := Iso.refl _`.

The localization's `tensorObj` is `((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj M).obj N`, which is
naturally isomorphic to (but not definitionally equal to) the `tensorObj` defined above
as `L.obj (F.obj M ⊗ F.obj N)`; both unfold to sheafification of the presheaf tensor. -/
noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) := by
  letI : MonoidalCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
    show MonoidalCategory
      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
      inferInstance
  exact inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
    (LocalizedMonoidal (sheafificationFunctor X) (W X) (Iso.refl _)))

/-- The pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms transfer from the presheaf side
through the localization-monoidal universal property. -/
noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategory :
    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) := by
  letI : MonoidalCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
    show MonoidalCategory
      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
      inferInstance
  exact inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory
    (LocalizedMonoidal (sheafificationFunctor X) (W X) (Iso.refl _)))

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# The symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
10	
11	Phase C step C0 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules
12	on a scheme $X$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure, with
13	tensor product given by sheafification of the presheaf tensor product and
14	tensor unit given by the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ itself.
15	
16	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Modules_Monoidal.tex`.
17	
18	## Status (iteration 077 — refactor-subagent scaffold)
19	
20	This file scaffolds the three signatures (`tensorObj`,
21	`instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, `instMonoidalCategory`) needed by Phase C
22	step C1 (refining `LineBundle` from `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to the
23	invertible-object definition). The bodies are `sorry` for the prover
24	rounds iter-078+ to fill, using:
25	
26	- `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` (presheaf-side structure)
27	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` (pointwise tensor)
28	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` (left adjoint to inclusion)
29	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` (the comparison data)
30	
31	## Mathematical content
32	
33	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M$ and $N$,
34	$$
35	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N
36	    := \mathrm{sheafify}(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}).
37	$$
38	The associator/unitors/braiding on the sheaf side are sheafifications of their
39	presheaf counterparts; the coherence axioms transfer via the universal
40	property of sheafification.
41	-/
42	
43	set_option autoImplicit false
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
50	
51	variable (X : Scheme.{u})
52	
53	/-- The tensor product of two $\mathcal O_X$-modules: sheafify the presheaf
54	tensor product of the underlying presheaves-of-modules.
55	
56	The instance lookup for `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules R₀)` requires
57	`R₀` to syntactically present as `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`. Since
58	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.val ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` only
59	definitionally (through `sheafCompose`), we use `show` to expose the form. -/
60	noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
61	  letI : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
62	    show MonoidalCategoryStruct
63	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
64	      inferInstance
65	  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
66	    ((Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj M ⊗ (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj N)
67	
68	/-- Bridging instance: `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj` carries the
69	`MonoidalCategory` structure inherited from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
70	through the definitional equality `X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ ...`.
71	Needed for `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal`, `LocalizedMonoidal`, and all downstream
72	constructions in this file to typecheck. -/
73	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryPresheaf :
74	    MonoidalCategory (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
75	  show MonoidalCategory
76	    (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
77	    inferInstance
78	
79	/-- The presheaf-to-sheaf sheafification functor for `X.Modules`. Abbreviated `L`
80	in comments. -/
81	noncomputable abbrev sheafificationFunctor :
82	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⥤ X.Modules :=
83	  _root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
84	
85	/-- The morphism property in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj` consisting of
86	morphisms inverted by sheafification. By `Adjunction.isLocalization` together with
87	fullness/faithfulness of the right adjoint of `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`,
88	the sheafification functor is a localization at this class. -/
89	noncomputable abbrev W : MorphismProperty
90	    (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
91	  (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms _).inverseImage (sheafificationFunctor X)
92	
93	/-- Sheafification is a localization at `W`. Follows from `Adjunction.isLocalization`
94	since the right adjoint of `sheafification (𝟙 _)` is Full + Faithful. -/
95	noncomputable instance sheafificationIsLocalization :
96	    (sheafificationFunctor X).IsLocalization (W X) :=
97	  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
98	    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isLocalization
99	
100	/-- GAP-FILL: sheafification of `PresheafOfModules` sends presheaf-tensor whiskering
101	of an iso-inverted morphism to an iso. This is the single semantic gap-fill
102	needed to invoke the `LocalizedMonoidal` machinery (`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`)
103	on the sheafification functor.
104	
105	Mathematical content. The class `W X` of morphisms in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
106	inverted by sheafification is stable under left- and right-whiskering by any presheaf-of-modules.
107	This holds because sheafification is a left adjoint to a fully-faithful right adjoint, and the
108	presheaf tensor product `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight` are colimit-preserving bifunctors on each
109	variable (see `PresheafOfModules.tensorLeft.PreservesColimitsOfSize` in
110	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean:237,243`).
111	
112	Closing this `IsMonoidal` instance below the level of `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk'` reduces
113	to the iso-stability of `sheafification` under presheaf-tensor whiskering, which is the analog
114	for `PresheafOfModules` of `GrothendieckTopology.W.monoidal` for fixed-value sheaves
115	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Monoidal.lean:149`). The latter is proved via internal-hom
116	adjunction; the `PresheafOfModules` version needs the same technique but adapted to the
117	varying-value-category setting — substantial Mathlib infrastructure not currently present.
118	Marked sorry until the upstream gap is filled. -/
119	noncomputable instance instIsMonoidal_W : (W X).IsMonoidal := by
120	  refine MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk' _ ?_
121	  intro X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂ f g hf hg
122	  -- `hf hg` say `sheafification f` and `sheafification g` are isos in `X.Modules`.
123	  -- Goal: `sheafification (f ⊗ₘ g)` is an iso. Substantive: needs iso-stability of
124	  -- sheafification under presheaf-tensor whiskering. See declaration docstring.
125	  sorry
126	
127	/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: derived from the
128	`LocalizedMonoidal` machinery applied to the sheafification functor
129	`L : PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⥤ X.Modules` at the class `W X` of morphisms
130	inverted by `L`, with unit `L.obj (𝟙_ presheaf)` and `ε := Iso.refl _`.
131	
132	The localization's `tensorObj` is `((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj M).obj N`, which is
133	naturally isomorphic to (but not definitionally equal to) the `tensorObj` defined above
134	as `L.obj (F.obj M ⊗ F.obj N)`; both unfold to sheafification of the presheaf tensor. -/
135	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
136	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) :=
137	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
138	    (LocalizedMonoidal (sheafificationFunctor X) (W X) (Iso.refl _)))
139	
140	/-- The pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms transfer from the presheaf side
141	through the localization-monoidal universal property. -/
142	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategory :
143	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) :=
144	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory
145	    (LocalizedMonoidal (sheafificationFunctor X) (W X) (Iso.refl _)))
146	
147	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

noncomputable instance instIsMonoidal_W : (W X).IsMonoidal := by
  -- We use `IsMonoidal.mk'`, splitting `f ⊗ₘ g = (f ▷ Y₁) ≫ (X₂ ◁ g)` and
  -- reducing to iso-stability of `sheafification` under presheaf-tensor whiskering.
  -- The substantive whiskering iso-stability remains a Mathlib gap; see docstring.
  refine MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal.mk' _ fun {X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂} f g hf hg => ?_
  simp only [MorphismProperty.inverseImage_iff,
    MorphismProperty.isomorphisms.iff] at hf hg ⊢
  rw [show f ⊗ₘ g = (f ▷ Y₁) ≫ (X₂ ◁ g) from MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_def f g,
    Functor.map_comp]
  -- Goal: IsIso (L.map (f ▷ Y₁) ≫ L.map (X₂ ◁ g)).
  -- Both factors are iso *if* W is monoidal — which is what we're proving.
  -- The substantive iso-stability of sheafification under presheaf-tensor whiskering
  -- is the actual gap (see declaration docstring).
  have h_whiskerRight : IsIso ((sheafificationFunctor X).map (f ▷ Y₁)) := by sorry
  have h_whiskerLeft : IsIso ((sheafificationFunctor X).map (X₂ ◁ g)) := by sorry
  exact IsIso.comp_isIso

### Why this is hard
Verified the following routes are blocked:

1. **Decomposition via `tensorHom_def`** (`f ⊗ₘ g = (f ▷ Y₁) ≫ (X₂ ◁ g)`): reduces to two
   instances of `L.map (h ▷ Z)` iso / `L.map (Z ◁ h)` iso when `L.map h` is iso. These are
   exactly the `IsMonoidal.whiskerLeft` and `IsMonoidal.whiskerRight` conditions — no progress.

2. **Reduction via `sheafificationCompToSheaf`**: `L.map h` iso iff
   `(presheafToSheaf AddCommGrpCat).map ((toPresheaf R₀).map h)` iso (since `SheafOfModules.toSheaf`
